papers.title_en: When LLMs Develop Languages: Symbolic Communication for Efficient Multi-Agent Reasoning
papers.authors: Zhengqi Pei, Qingming Huang, Shuhui Wang
papers.evidence: 通过符号通信实现多智能体LLM推理用于任务自动化
papers.tldr: 大语言模型在复杂推理任务中常用思维链方法，但生成冗长自然语言，效率低。本文提出CLSR框架，让多个LLM智能体自主发明、演化并共享紧凑的符号语言体系（LSF），通过无潜在路由自适应选择语言组合，在保证推理准确率的同时将生成token数减少3-6倍。该方法将每个LSF视为可复用的符号协议，包含符号、规则和消息传递契约，通过基于正确性和token成本的进化循环改进。实验证明CLSR在多个基准上显著降低推理延迟，并理论上推导出任意符号体系下token成本的信息论下界。
papers.abstract_en: Chain-of-Thought (CoT) improves large language models (LLMs) on difficult reasoning tasks, but it often incurs long natural-language rationales that are poorly aligned with efficient machine reasoning. We propose Communicative Language Symbolism Routing (CLSR), a test-time framework in which multiple LLM agents autonomously invent, evolve, and share compact Language Symbolism Frameworks (LSFs), while a latent-free router adaptively selects and composes these languages per query to optimize the accuracy-token trade-off. Unlike prompt optimization that refines surface instructions, CLSR treats each LSF as a reusable symbolic protocol with compact symbols, usage rules, and a message-passing contract, and improves it through an evolutionary loop driven by correctness and token cost. At inference time, the router may invoke a single low-cost LSF call, ensemble multiple LSFs, or execute a multi-round LSF composition protocol on harder queries. Across challenging benchmarks, CLSR reduces latency-oriented generated token completion by $3\sim 6\times$ compared to standard CoT while maintaining accuracy. We further derive an information-theoretic lower bound on token cost under arbitrary symbolism and show that, under an interpreter-realizability premise, multi-round LSF protocols conditionally subsume program-execution pipelines. Code is publicly available (https://github.com/pzqpzq/LSF_MDia).

papers.title_en: A3M: Adaptive, Adversarial and Multi-Objective Learning for Strategic Bidding in Repeated Auctions
papers.authors: Junhan Li, Yuxin Zhang, Haoran Wang, Minghao Chen
papers.evidence: 自适应深度强化学习与多目标奖励设计用于拍卖出价
papers.tldr: 重复多单位拍卖中投标学习面临探索-利用平衡、对手非平稳和目标单一等挑战。A3M框架集成自适应深度强化学习、显式对抗推理和多目标奖励设计，在歧视性和统一价格拍卖中遗憾降低30-40%，对策略转移稳健且可调多目标。消融验证各组件必要性，为复杂拍卖提供灵活优化框架。
papers.abstract_en: Learning to bid in repeated multi-unit auctions with bandit feedback poses a fundamental challenge. Existing methods often rely on rigid explore-then-exploit schedules, assume stationary adversaries, and optimize solely for bidder utility, thereby limiting adaptability and strategic robustness. To address these limitations, we introduce the A3M framework, which integrates adaptive deep reinforcement learning (DRL), explicit adversarial reasoning, and principled multi-objective reward design for online auction strategy optimization. A3M employs an actor-critic DRL backbone to dynamically balance exploration and exploitation, an opponent model for fictitious play against non-stationary adversaries, and a composite reward function to jointly maximize utility, auctioneer revenue, and fairness. We provide the first comprehensive empirical evaluation of this integrated approach against established baselines in both discriminatory and uniform price auctions. Results show that A3M reduces final regret by 30--40\% in standard settings, maintains robust performance against adversarial strategy shifts, scales favorably with the number of units $K$, and enables tunable multi-objective trade-offs. An extensive ablation study confirms the necessity of each core component. Our work establishes A3M as a powerful and flexible framework for learning in complex auction environments.
